label: PostHog
description: Query events, persons, cohorts, and feature flags from your PostHog project.

form.title: Connect PostHog
form.subtitle: Anton uses your personal API key to query events and run insights against your PostHog project.

methods.label: Personal API key
methods.description: Paste a Personal API key from your PostHog account. Stored locally — never sent anywhere except PostHog itself.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a PostHog Personal API key

Personal API keys give Anton your account-level scopes — they can read across every project you have access to (you can also scope them down). Project IDs and host vary by where your PostHog instance lives.

### Steps

1. Sign in to PostHog at [us.posthog.com](https://us.posthog.com), [eu.posthog.com](https://eu.posthog.com), or your self-hosted URL.
2. Click your profile icon (top right) → **Account settings**.
3. Open the **Personal API keys** tab.
4. Click **+ Create personal API key**. Name it `Anton`, optionally restrict the scopes to read-only or specific projects.
5. Copy the generated key (starts with `phx_…`). Paste it into **Personal API key** below.
6. Find your **Project ID** — it's the number in the URL when you're inside a project, e.g. `us.posthog.com/project/12345/dashboard` → project ID is `12345`.
7. Pick the **Host** that matches where you signed in (US Cloud, EU Cloud, or paste your self-hosted URL).

### Notes

- The Personal API key is a credential — anyone with it can read everything you can read in PostHog. Anton stores it locally; rotate it from the same Account settings panel if you suspect exposure.
- Project-scoped Project API keys (the `phc_…` ones used by SDK ingestion) are NOT what you want here — those can only WRITE events, not read them.
- Read [PostHog's API authentication docs](https://posthog.com/docs/api#how-to-obtain-a-personal-api-key).

fields.name: personal_api_key
fields.label: Personal API key
fields.description: Starts with phx_ — generate from PostHog → Account settings → Personal API keys.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Match the region you signed in to.

fields.name: custom_host
fields.label: Self-hosted URL
fields.description: Only fill in if you picked Self-hosted above.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project ID
fields.description: The number in your project URL: us.posthog.com/project/<project_id>/…
